chief_complaint: An 80-day-old pig presents with polyserositis, meningoencephalitis, arthritis, and omphalitis.
history: Bacterial polyserositis, arthritis, and meningitis are well-known conditions in pigs, commonly linked to Glaesserella parasuis, Streptococcus suis, and Mycoplasma hyorhinis. This study reports a rare case of Salmonella enterica subsp. enterica serovar Agona (S. Agona) infection.
exam: Physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Bacteriological examination revealed a pure culture of S. Agona from fibrinous lesions, and immunohistochemical examination confirmed the septicaemic distribution of the bacteria.
correct_diagnosis: Salmonella enterica subsp. enterica serovar Agona (S. Agona) infection
differentials: Salmonella enterica subsp. enterica serovar Agona (S. Agona) infection
correct_ranking: Salmonella enterica subsp. enterica serovar Agona (S. Agona) infection
